titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Ravi D. Goel Collection on Yale
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid written in English.
CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0)

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 20, 2020 at 07:00

did.unittitle: Ravi D. Goel collection on Yale
did.abstract: The collection consist of letters, postcards, photograph albums, and various printed ephemera documenting Yale affiliated individuals and organizations collected by Ravi D. Goel.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 45 (YRG 45), Yale objects and memorabilia.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Ravi D. Goel (Yale College, 1993), 2008-2018.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ravi D. Goel Collection on Yale (RU 1081). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

odd.head: General
odd.p: The collection was formerly titled Yale letters and memorabilia collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consist of letters, postcards, photograph albums, and various printed ephemera documenting Yale affiliated individuals and organizations collected by Ravi D. Goel.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged by accession, and chronologically therein.

did.unittitle: Yale letters and memorabilia

descriptivenote.p: Yale letters and memorabilia

did.unittitle: R.S. Corring letter to Alder Scorch

descriptivenote.p: R.S. Corring letter to Alder Scorch

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: E.A. Bradford (Yale 1833) letter to his mother Lois Bradford

descriptivenote.p: E.A. Bradford (Yale 1833) letter to his mother Lois Bradford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter describes a visit to New Haven by President Jackson, Vice President Van Buren, Secretary of War Cass, Secretary of the Navy Woodbury, and Governor of New York Marcy.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Anthony D. Stanley (Yale 1830) letter recommending Osbert Burr Loomis (Yale 1835) for a position in the Department of Sciences

descriptivenote.p: Anthony D. Stanley (Yale 1830) letter recommending Osbert Burr Loomis (Yale 1835) for a position in the Department of Sciences

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Joanna B., Mrs., letter to George Duffield (Yale 1837)

descriptivenote.p: Joanna B., Mrs., letter to George Duffield (Yale 1837)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include a Hymn, religion, the King of England, and marriage.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First Chinese student writes of student life, Munson Academy, and "The many disadvantages with which I labor."

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Timothy Cutler letter to Sir Geflow Cambridge

descriptivenote.p: Timothy Cutler letter to Sir Geflow Cambridge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Cambridge University colleague (President?) regarding his son and which college he will chose to go to; Christopher Bridge a recent graduate of Harvard, enjoying some Madeira wine, the comments of Sir Isaac Newton and procurement of books from a fellow at Eton College.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Mary Welch indenture document signed by David Lee and Ebenezer Gray

descriptivenote.p: Mary Welch indenture document signed by David Lee and Ebenezer Gray

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The doument concerns the indenture of Mary Welch, an 8 year old poor female whose father, Jonathan Welch of Lebanon, is deceased. Mary is to be a Maid Servant or Apprentice to David Lee (1674 - 1759) and his wife of Coventry, Connecticut, until she reached the age of 18. Document stipulates Mary's duties and the moral behavior by which she must abide. The document further stipulates the obligations of the Lee family to provide well for Mary, to teach her to read, sew, and to perform the tasks of a Maid Servant, which consisted of standard housekeeping tasks of the day.
Jonathan Trumble [Trumbull] is mentioned in the document.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: List of contents with detailed descriptions by Ravi D. Goel

descriptivenote.p: List of contents with detailed descriptions by Ravi D. Goel

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Jonathan Trumble (Trumbull) document from court of Justice Isaac Huntington

descriptivenote.p: Jonathan Trumble (Trumbull) document from court of Justice Isaac Huntington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Document from the court of Justice Isaac Huntington of Norwich, Connecticut. Signed by Revolutionary War patriot Jonathan Trumble [Trumbull] with the form of spelling in use for the family's name until 1766. This document was sent to the Sheriff of New London County ordering him to summon Joshua Woodworth to court to answer to Thomas Danforth for debts owed. Sheriff's deposition and account of court costs on reverse.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Ebenezer Silliman (Yale 1727) directive to Nathaniel Stanley

descriptivenote.p: Ebenezer Silliman (Yale 1727) directive to Nathaniel Stanley

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter directs Stanley to issue payment due to Daniel Edwards (Yale 1725)

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Thaddeus Betts (Yale 1745), Justice of the Peace in Norwalk, letter to Treasurer of State of Connecticut

descriptivenote.p: Thaddeus Betts (Yale 1745), Justice of the Peace in Norwalk, letter to Treasurer of State of Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Abatement of 63 pounds is issued for overtaxation in 1776.

descriptivenote.p: Elizur Goodrich (Yale 1779), Probate Court Judge for New Haven, legal document

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The document appoints William M'Cracken and Nathan Smith as executors for William M'Cracken's estate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: J. J. Edic (Yale 1864) letter to his brother

descriptivenote.p: J. J. Edic (Yale 1864) letter to his brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a brief mention of classmate (Thomas Carolin Clay) who "left yesterday on account of the secession he was from Georgia"; rest of letter relates issues of daily life.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Timothy Dwight letter to Professor Hewitt

descriptivenote.p: Timothy Dwight letter to Professor Hewitt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the letter Dwight asks Hewitt to preach at the First Congregational Church of Milford in August.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Theodore Dwight Woolsey (Yale 1820) letter to Carl Stoeckel

descriptivenote.p: Theodore Dwight Woolsey (Yale 1820) letter to Carl Stoeckel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woolsey discusses a book missing from his personal library (Edward Buck's "Massachusetts ecclesiastical law" (1866) and the inability of colonial ministers to unite a couple in marriage and the post-1700 law allowing ministers to unite, with magistrates present.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Timothy Dwight (Yale 1849) condolence letter to Mrs. Littleton P. Franklin

descriptivenote.p: Timothy Dwight (Yale 1849) condolence letter to Mrs. Littleton P. Franklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Condolence letter from Yale President Timothy Dwight to the widow of his classmate Littleton P. Franklin (Yale 1849). Dwight writes of remembering his classmate from college days.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

did.unittitle: Timothy Dwight (Yale 1849) letter to Mrs. Littleton P. Franklin

descriptivenote.p: Timothy Dwight (Yale 1849) letter to Mrs. Littleton P. Franklin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Yale President Timothy Dwight to the widow of his classmate Littleton P. Franklin (Yale 1849), in which Dwight requestsfor biographical information for upcoming 40th reunion of the Yale Class of 1849.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies

did.unittitle: Yale letters and memorabilia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The accession consists of letters and legal documents documenting Yale affiliated individuals and organizations collected by Ravi D. Goel.

did.unittitle: Ebenezer Gray writ to sheriff of Windham County, Connecticut
Abraham Pierson letter to constable of Killingworth, Connecticut
Sermon by Reverend Jacob Eliot

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related Material: Jacob Eliot family papers (MS 193)

did.unittitle: Deed to Joseph Backus (Yale 1718)
Benjamin Wright deed to James Baker, signed by John Russell (Yale 1704) and Samuel Malthie (Yale 1712)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Russell was the son of Yale co-founder Samuel Russell.

did.unittitle: Shubael Conant (Yale 1732) letter concerning deed of Timothy Dimmock
Timothy Pierce Jr. promissory note, signed by Thomas Stevens and David Jewell
Jonathan Trumble (Trumbull) summons to constable of Norwich, New London County, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Trumble" is an early spelling for Connecticut Governor Jonathan Trumbull (1710-1785)

did.unittitle: Saybrook, Connecticut deed signed by Ambrose Whittlesey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ambrose Whittlesey (1712-1756) was the husband of Elizabeth (Mather) Whittlesey, granddaughter of Yale co-founder Reverend Samuel Mather.

did.unittitle: Sermon by Reverend Naphtali Daggett
New London court document signed by clerk Daniel Edwards (Yale 1720)
Isaac Huntington deed concerning lands of James Fitch
Eliphalet Dyer (Yale 1740) letter to Jedediah Elderton
Deed signed by Edmund Fanning (Yale 1757), North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edmund Fanning was a Loyalist who was credited with saving Yale from destruction by British forces during the 1779 invasion of New Haven. Fanning was awarded an honorary degree of Doctor of Laws by Yale College and Dartmouth College in 1803.

did.unittitle: Daniel Edwards (Yale 1720) letter to James Church (Yale 1756)
Samuel Wood letter to Eliza Smith, regarding the death of Yale President Thomas Clap
Benjamin Stiles (Yale 1740) summons to the sheriff of Litchfield County, Connecticut
Jabez Huntington (Yale 1741), testimony of Joseph Sanford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also signed by Joseph Sanford and Richard Hide, Justice of the Peace for New London County, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Eliphalet Dyer (Yale 1740) and Samuel Gray, note of credit for services of an enslaved person named Cuff
Benjamin Stiles (Yale 1740) summons
Promissory note signed by Eleazer Wales (Yale 1783), Samuel Lyman (Yale 1770), Samuel Wyllys, and John Lawrence
Samuel Hall property deed signed by Jabez Hamlin
Eliphalet Dyer (Yale 1740) property agreement between James Keith and John Lawrence
Eleazer Wales (Yale 1753) record of land sold
Stephen Titus Hosmer (Yale 1782) signed summons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stephen Titus Hosmer served as chief justice of the Connecticut Supreme Court (1815-1833) and was awarded an honorary Legum Doctor degree from Yale in 1823.

did.unittitle: Henry Daggett (Yale 1771) or Henry Daggett Jr. (Yale 1775) and Daniel Lyman (Yale 1745), letter to Silas Hotchkiss
John Davenport (Yale 1770) letter to Peter T. Curtenius
Matthew Talcott Russell (Yale 1779) summons to the sheriff of Middlesex County, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Matthew Talcott Russell was the father of William Huntington Russell (Yale 1833), the founder of the Skull and Bones society. He was also the descendant of Reverend Noadiah Russell (1659-1713), one of the first trustees of Yale.

did.unittitle: State of Connecticut promissory note to Charles Phelps, signed by John Lawrence, Eleazer Wales (Yale 1783), and Jon Bull (Yale 1765)
George Pitkin letter to John Lawrence, concerning a prisoner convicted of burglary
Asa Spalding letter concerning the estate of Jonathan Trumbull
Joshua Elderkin (Yale 1748) letter to Oliver Wolcott Jr. (Yale 1778)
Samuel Whiting letter to an unidentified recipient

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter mentions "Mr. Wolcott," likely Oliver Wolcott Jr. (Yale 1778), comptroller of the Connecticut Treasury appointed in 1791. Wolcott's father Oliver Wolcott Sr. (Yale 1747) was a signer of the Declaration of Independence.

did.unittitle: James Kent (Yale 1781) indenture
Yale treasurer James Hillhouse (Yale 1773) manuscript on relations between Yale and State of Connecticut
Captain Ebenezer Smith letter to his son David Smith (Yale 1795)
Asa Stratton land deal signed by Obadiah Dickinson (Yale 1778) and Humphrey Atherton Champney
Samuel Johnson (Yale 1779) letter to James Hillhouse (Yale 1773)
Captain Ebenezer Smith letter to his son David Smith (Yale 1795)
Benjamin Stiles (Yale 1740) deed
Benjamin Gilbert deed to William S. Smith, signed by New York Supreme Court Chief Justice James Kent (Yale 1781)
John Treadwell (Yale 1767) payment record
William Hillhouse letter to Andrew Kingsbury, Connecticut state treasurer
Stephen Titus Hosmer (Yale 1782) signed summons
Andrew Kingsbury letter to Jonathan Trumbull Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jonathan Trumbull Jr. served as the 20th Governor of Connecticut from 1797 to 1809.

did.unittitle: Amos Benedict (Yale 1800) letter to Thomas Lewis
Samuel Danielson (Yale 1764) land transfer to his son
John Chester (Yale 1766) letter to an unidentified recipient
